Environment facts:
- First collect Debian release, SSH access path, firewall rules, current Lynis findings, sysctl files, and whether out-of-band console recovery exists.

Evidence:
- Run `scripts/security-audit-probe.sh`, `scripts/network-diagnose.sh`, and `scripts/risk-check.sh -- "apply all Lynis hardening automatically"`.
- Review SSH, PAM, profile, and sysctl settings as observed facts before changing them.

Safe next checks:
- Do not apply all Lynis warnings automatically on a remote SSH-only host. Prioritize changes by lockout risk and keep a second SSH session open.

Proposed changes:
- Requires user approval for each stage. Back up every changed file, use a timed rollback for firewall or SSH changes, verify login and service health, and rollback immediately from the saved configs if access degrades.
